items.title: Noah Kahan Concertgoers Are Apparently Still Using Their Seats As Toilets
items.summary: Noah Kahan's The Great Divide Tour kicked off last month, and there has been no shortage of controversy. The Philadelphia show on June 26 made headlines after an attendee went number two in their seat, and the folk-pop star made fans at subsequent shows pledge not to shit on the floor. Now, it seems like the problem is worse than we thought.
items.audio: audio/Noah Kahan Concertgoers Are Apparently Still Using.mp3

items.title: Radio Free Alice – “Kick In The Shins”
items.summary: Melbourne indie rockers Radio Free Alice have a sound that recalls the prime blog-rock days of the early '00s. It's a tense and loud but ultimately accessible version of post-punk, with big hooks belted out in in a desperate nasal-drone voice. If you're the right age, this stuff will instantly sweep you back through time, a powerfully pleasant sensation.
items.audio: audio/Radio Free Alice _ _Kick In The Shins.mp3

items.title: Charli XCX – “Camera”
items.summary: We have only a few more days until the release of Charli XCX's highly anticipated Brat followup Music, Fashion, Film. So far the pop star has shared the singles "Rock Music," "SS26," and "Wink Wink," along with the B-sides "I Keep On Thinking Bout You Every Single Day and Night" and "Playboy Bunny." She debuted a bunch of tracks in Brooklyn the other weekend, including "Camera," which is out now.

items.title: Peter Keys: An Artist’s Journey Across Multiple Musical Landscapes
items.summary: Peter Keys has crossed the global stage to find himself amidst the intimate soundscapes of Nashville. For most musicians, the goal is often the next gig, the next tour, or the next big event. For Peter Keys, it’s always been about something smaller and yet far more profound: being there. He calls it “living in […]
